tokamaker-jax research workbench

Static GitHub Pages companion for the tokamaker-jax solver stack. It mirrors the CLI, NiceGUI, validation, plotting, benchmark, differentiability, and upstream-comparison workflows in a browser-only research interface for MHD students and researchers.

static HTML research tabs TOML handoff validated runs in Python
Residual proxy
psi range
Mesh
Coil flux max
Gradients
Gate status

Case controls

0.1.0a0 static workbench

Workflow overview

same research lanes as the CLI and NiceGUI dashboard

Capability map

static status

Research handoff

reproducible
  • Explore: use the tabs to inspect a browser-side reduced model and the relevant equations.
  • Validate: run the generated CLI commands for JAX solves, differentiability checks, and parity gates.
  • Publish: export TOML, JSON manifest, benchmark records, and figures from the Python path.

Fixed-boundary equilibrium

Grad-Shafranov seed preview

Equation

Delta* psi = -mu0 R^2 p'(psi) - 0.5 F F'(psi) + S_coil

Solver path

Browser relaxation mirrors the fixed-boundary seed. Use the Python CLI for validated JAX solves and saved artifacts.

Outputs

Flux arrays, residual histories, figure recipes, PNG/GIF outputs, and config diagnostics.

Geometry and mesh

regions, rectangular seed grid, p=1 FEM topology

Mesh diagnostics

TriMesh

Nodes, cells, boundary markers, region ids, and OFT-style fixture inventory are tracked by the Python package.

Regions

Plasma, wall, limiter, and coil regions are TOML-serializable and plotted for reproducible geometry review.

Generalization

Topology policies for production mesh generation move into the next milestone; this tab keeps the interface ready.

Profiles and source terms

pressure, FF prime, current, and nonlinear iteration

Source balance

current settings

Nonlinear profiles

Profile iteration uses pressure and FF prime source terms with residual and sensitivity checks.

Literature plots

Pressure sweeps, CPC seed-family artifacts, and publication validation panels are generated from Python.

Inputs

Use TOML for grids, solver settings, source scaling, coils, regions, output paths, and dtype.

Coils and Green functions

free-boundary coupling preview

Coil-response diagnostics

elliptic and reduced gates

Circular loop

Closed-form elliptic Green function is checked against high-resolution quadrature in Python.

Free boundary

Coil boundary flux drives the profile-coupled equilibrium gate and current gradients.

OFT parity

When OpenFUSIONToolkit is available locally, `eval_green` parity records numeric code-to-code evidence.

Validation gates

physics gates and literature anchors

Gate matrix

GateCheckStatus

Command

tokamaker-jax verify --gate all --subdivisions 4 8 16

Differentiability and sensitivities

JAX gradient contract

Gradient checks

browser proxy values

AD policy

Local FEM kernels, assembly paths, coil kernels, and profile scales are designed for JAX transforms.

Research use

Use gradients for optimization, profile fitting, inverse design, and control-oriented studies.

Next milestone

Implicit solver VJPs and topology-aware derivatives remain explicitly tracked future work.

Performance and benchmark history

threshold-aware CI artifacts

Benchmark lanes

normalized to baseline

CI artifacts

Benchmark JSON, threshold reports, and history helpers are produced by the repository workflow.

Hot paths

Seed solve, p=1 FEM kernels, global assembly, matrix-free apply, and coil responses are tracked separately.

Interpretation

Browser values are normalized proxies; publish numbers from `examples/benchmark_report.py`.

Export and reproducibility

copy into the Python/JAX workflow

TOML case

CLI commands

Artifact manifest

Research checklist

before publication
  • Run physics gates: verify all selected gates and save JSON outputs.
  • Compare: record upstream fixture, OFT parity, or literature reproduction status.
  • Benchmark: attach benchmark threshold report and environment metadata.
  • Archive: keep TOML, generated figures, command log, and exact package version together.
